mission.title: kube-rs: Add backoff to watcher reconnection
mission.description: Add backoff to watcher reconnection. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Check kube-rs project setup
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kube-rs deployment:
```bash
cargo --version
rustc --version
grep kube Cargo.toml 2>/dev/null
```
Check the kube-rs dependency version in your Rust project.

steps.title: Review kube-rs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-rs configuration:
```bash
grep -A5 '\[dependencies\]' Cargo.toml
cargo tree | grep kube
```
Currently `watcher` will always keep a reconnection attempt in-flight when polled (and there is no active connection already). That's a bit excessive, and pretty unkind to the apiserver.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add backoff to watcher reconnection
steps.description:
First moves the imports of `kube_runtime` reflector around a bit first as per comments in https://github.com/kube-rs/kube-rs/issues/577 (https://github.com/kube-rs/kube-rs/issues/577#issuecomment-958974973):

- `runtime::reflector` module renamed to `runtime::cache` module
- `store` module within `cache` made private, but re-exporting `Writer` (as well) from the module

Then we add a slightly more ergonomic builder struct `Cache<K>` that wraps `reflector`.
This encapsulates the `Writer` and

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Add backoff to watcher reconnection is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
cargo build 2>&1 | tail -20
cargo test 2>&1 | tail -20
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

- `runtime::reflector` module renamed to `runtime::cache` module
- `store` module within `cache` made private, but re-exporting `Writer` (as well) from the module

prerequisites.description: A Rust development environment with cargo and the kube-rs crate as a project dependency.
